game_overview: Lords of Waterdeep is a strategy board game set in the Dungeons & Dragons Forgotten Realms universe. Players take on the roles of secret Lords vying for control of Waterdeep, the City of Splendors. You recruit Adventurers to complete Quests, build new establishments, and use Intrigue to gain the upper hand against your rivals. Your Lord identity is secret, granting bonus VP for completing certain types of Quests.
objective: Score the most Victory Points (VP) by completing Quests, constructing Buildings, playing Intrigue cards, and fulfilling your Lord's secret bonus conditions.

components_overview.name: Game Board
components_overview.description: The city of Waterdeep with 9 basic Buildings (action spaces), the rounds track (1-8), the scoring track, and 10 open spaces for advanced Building tiles.

components_overview.name: Agents
components_overview.description: Wooden agent tokens in each player color. Each player starts with 4 Agents in their pool. Each player also places 1 Agent near the Round 5 space.

components_overview.name: Agents
components_overview.description: Wooden agent tokens in each player color. Each player starts with 3 Agents in their pool. Each player also places 1 Agent near the Round 5 space.

components_overview.name: Agents
components_overview.description: Wooden agent tokens in each player color. Each player starts with 2 Agents in their pool. Each player also places 1 Agent near the Round 5 space.

components_overview.name: Adventurer Cubes
components_overview.description: 100 cubes in four types: Clerics (white, 25), Fighters (orange, 25), Rogues (black, 25), Wizards (purple, 25). Used to complete Quests. If the supply runs out of a type, you cannot gain more of that type.

components_overview.name: Quest Cards
components_overview.description: 60 cards showing adventurer requirements, gold cost, rewards, and Quest type (Arcana, Commerce, Piety, Skullduggery, Warfare). Completed Quests score VP. Some are Plot Quests with ongoing effects.

components_overview.name: Intrigue Cards
components_overview.description: 50 special action cards played at Waterdeep Harbor. Three types: Attack (harm opponents), Utility (help yourself), Mandatory Quest (force an opponent to complete a minor Quest before any others).

components_overview.name: Lord Cards
components_overview.description: 11 secret identity cards. Each Lord scores bonus VP at game end for completing specific Quest types or controlling Buildings.

components_overview.name: Building Tiles
components_overview.description: 24 advanced Building tiles that add new action spaces when purchased from Builder's Hall. Cost varies by tile. Owner gains a benefit when any other player uses the Building.

components_overview.name: Building Control Markers
components_overview.description: Die-cut tokens matching each player color, placed on Buildings to indicate ownership. 9 per player color.

components_overview.name: Gold Tokens
components_overview.description: Currency used to buy Buildings, pay Quest costs, and activate certain actions. 50 tokens of 1 Gold and 10 tokens of 5 Gold.

components_overview.name: Victory Point Tokens
components_overview.description: 36 VP tokens placed on the rounds track at setup (3 per round space). At the start of each round, the 3 tokens from that round's space are distributed to face-up Buildings in Builder's Hall. Also 5 tokens marked 100 VP for when a score marker laps the track.

components_overview.name: Ambassador
components_overview.description: Neutral Agent piece. Gained by assigning an Agent to the Palace of Waterdeep advanced Building. You assign the Ambassador before any other player at the start of the next round, then it returns when that round ends.

components_overview.name: Lieutenant
components_overview.description: Neutral Agent piece. Gained permanently by completing the Recruit Lieutenant Plot Quest. Once recruited, the Lieutenant counts as one of your Agents for the rest of the game.

components_overview.name: First Player Marker
components_overview.description: Indicates who goes first each round. Obtained by assigning an Agent to Castle Waterdeep.

components_overview.name: Score Markers
components_overview.description: 5 circular wooden pieces (one per player color) for the scoring track.

components_overview.name: Player Mats
components_overview.description: 5 mats (one per player color) with areas for Agent Pool, Tavern (holds Adventurers and Gold), and Completed Quests.

components_overview.name: Corruption Track
components_overview.description: Tracks Corruption tokens taken by players. At game end, players lose VP based on total Corruption in play.

components_overview.name: Corruption Tokens
components_overview.description: Negative VP tokens gained from certain powerful actions. The more Corruption in play, the more VP each token costs at game end.

components_overview.name: Skullport Board
components_overview.description: Expansion board with powerful action spaces that often give Corruption as a cost.

components_overview.name: Undermountain Board
components_overview.description: Expansion board with high-reward action spaces for experienced players.

setup.description: Unfold and place the game board in the center of the play area within easy reach of all players.
Sort the Adventurer cubes by color (white Clerics, orange Fighters, black Rogues, purple Wizards) and place them in the supply near the board. Also place Gold tokens in the supply.
Place 3 Victory Point tokens on each space of the rounds track.
Shuffle the Building tiles and draw 3. Place them face-up in the 3 spaces of Builder's Hall. The remaining tiles form a face-down stack placed on the labeled space near Builder's Hall.
Shuffle the Quest cards and place them face-down as the Quest deck on the labeled space near Cliffwatch Inn.
Shuffle the Intrigue cards and place them face-down as the Intrigue deck on the labeled space near Waterdeep Harbor.
Each player chooses a color and takes: a player mat, all Agents of that color, the score marker, and Building control markers of that color.
Place each player's score marker on space 0 of the scoring track.
Each player starts with 4 Agents in their Agent Pool. Place 1 additional Agent of your color near the Round 5 space of the rounds track.
Each player starts with 3 Agents in their Agent Pool. Place 1 additional Agent of your color near the Round 5 space of the rounds track.
Each player starts with 2 Agents in their Agent Pool. Place 1 additional Agent of your color near the Round 5 space of the rounds track.
Shuffle the Lord cards and deal 1 face-down to each player. Look at your Lord secretly and keep it hidden. Return the remaining Lord cards to the box face-down.
Deal 2 Quest cards face-up to each player. These form each player's active Quests. Then place 1 face-up Quest card in each of the 4 spaces of Cliffwatch Inn.
Deal 2 Intrigue cards face-down to each player. Keep these hidden from opponents.
The player who has most recently been to another city goes first. Give that player the First Player marker.
The starting player gets 4 Gold. Each subsequent player (going left) receives 1 more Gold than the player to their right. (1st: 4 Gold, 2nd: 5 Gold, 3rd: 6 Gold, 4th: 7 Gold, 5th: 8 Gold.)
Place the Corruption Track near the board. Fill all spaces with Corruption tokens from the supply.
Place the Skullport and/or Undermountain expansion boards next to the main board.

gameplay.turn_structure: The game is played over 8 rounds. Each round, players take turns placing 1 Agent at a time on an unoccupied action space and immediately taking that action. After placing an Agent, a player may complete 1 Quest. When all Agents are assigned, Agents at Waterdeep Harbor are reassigned, then the round ends and all Agents return to their pools.

actions.name: Assign Agent
actions.description: On your turn, place 1 of your available Agents on any unoccupied action space and immediately take that action.

steps.description: Take 1 Agent from your pool.
Place it on any unoccupied action space of any Building (basic or advanced) on the board.
Immediately take the action shown on that space (just once).
If the space is on a Building owned by another player, that owner gains the benefit stated in the tile's Owner line.

notes.description: You cannot place an Agent on an action space that already contains another Agent (yours or another player's).
You cannot choose to pass. If you have Agents available, you must assign 1.
In the unlikely event that you cannot take any action, you must pass.
If you have no Agents left to assign, you cannot take a turn. Play proceeds to the next player with available Agents.

actions.name: Complete a Quest
actions.description: After assigning (or reassigning) an Agent, you may complete 1 Quest if you have the required Adventurers and Gold.

steps.description: Choose an active Quest card from in front of you.
Remove the required Adventurer cubes and Gold from your Tavern and return them to the supply.
Collect the reward shown on the Quest card (VP, resources, cards, etc.).
Place the completed Quest face-down on the Completed Quests area of your player mat.
If it is a Plot Quest, place it face-up near your player mat as a reminder of its ongoing effect.

notes.description: You may complete only 1 Quest per turn, regardless of how many Agents you assign or reassign.
You can complete a Quest you just obtained this turn.
Mandatory Quests must be completed before any other Quests.
When a Mandatory Quest is completed, place the card in the Intrigue discard pile (not your completed Quests).
Plot Quests provide an ongoing effect in addition to their reward. The effect applies for the rest of the game.
Completing a Quest is optional -- you do not have to complete one.

actions.name: Cliffwatch Inn
actions.description: Cliffwatch Inn has 3 separate action spaces. You may assign more than 1 of your Agents here across different turns if spaces remain open.

steps.description: Space 1: Take 1 face-up Quest card from Cliffwatch Inn and take 2 Gold from the supply.
Space 2: Take 1 face-up Quest card from Cliffwatch Inn and draw 1 Intrigue card.
Space 3: Discard all face-up Quest cards from Cliffwatch Inn. Draw and place 4 new Quest cards from the Quest deck, then take 1 of the new face-up Quest cards.

notes.description: Whenever you take a face-up Quest card, immediately replace it with a new card from the Quest deck.
You can assign multiple Agents here on different turns as long as open spaces remain (still only 1 Agent per turn).

actions.name: Aurora's Realms Shop
actions.description: Take 4 Gold from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

steps.description: Take 4 Gold from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

actions.name: Blackstaff Tower
actions.description: Take 1 Wizard from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

steps.description: Take 1 Wizard (purple) from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

actions.name: The Plinth
actions.description: Take 1 Cleric from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

steps.description: Take 1 Cleric (white) from the supply and place it in your Tavern.

actions.name: The Grinning Lion Tavern
actions.description: Take 2 Rogues from the supply and place them in your Tavern.

steps.description: Take 2 Rogues (black) from the supply and place them in your Tavern.

actions.name: Field of Triumph
actions.description: Take 2 Fighters from the supply and place them in your Tavern.

steps.description: Take 2 Fighters (orange) from the supply and place them in your Tavern.

actions.name: Castle Waterdeep
actions.description: Take the First Player marker and draw 1 Intrigue card.

steps.description: Take the First Player marker (you will go first next round).
Draw 1 Intrigue card from the Intrigue deck.

actions.name: Builder's Hall
actions.description: Purchase a Building tile and put it into play under your control.

steps.description: Choose 1 of the face-up Building tiles available for purchase.
Pay its Gold cost to the supply.
Immediately score any VP shown on the Building tile.
Place the tile on one of the open Building spaces on the game board.
Place one of your Building control markers on the tile to show you are the owner.
Draw the top tile of the Building stack and place it face-up in the vacated space in Builder's Hall.

notes.description: Buildings in play are available for anyone to assign an Agent to, just like basic Buildings.
When another player assigns an Agent to your Building, you gain the Owner benefit stated on the tile.
You do NOT gain the Owner benefit when you yourself use your own Building.
Some Buildings have 'When Purchased and at Start of Round' effects that place resources on the tile.

actions.name: Waterdeep Harbor
actions.description: Waterdeep Harbor has 3 action spaces. Assigning an Agent here lets you play 1 Intrigue card. At the end of the round, Agents here are reassigned to other action spaces.

steps.description: Play 1 Intrigue card from your hand and follow its instructions immediately.
If you have no Intrigue cards in hand, you cannot assign an Agent to Waterdeep Harbor.

notes.description: You may assign more than 1 of your Agents to Waterdeep Harbor across different turns if spaces remain (still 1 per turn).
After ALL other Agents have been assigned for the round, Agents at Waterdeep Harbor are reassigned in order (space 1, then 2, then 3).
Each reassigned Agent is moved to any other unoccupied action space (not Waterdeep Harbor) and the player takes that action.
After reassigning, you may complete 1 Quest as usual.
Attack Intrigue cards harm or penalize opponents. Utility cards benefit you. Mandatory Quest cards force an opponent to complete a small Quest before any others.

actions.name: Start of Round
actions.description: At the start of each round, resolve these steps before players take turns.

steps.description: Remove the 3 VP tokens from the current round's space on the rounds track.
Place 1 of those VP tokens on each face-up Building tile in Builder's Hall. (VP tokens accumulate on tiles over multiple rounds.)
Resolve any 'Start of Round' effects on advanced Buildings currently in play (e.g., buildings that place resources on their space).
At the start of Round 5 only: each player takes the extra Agent of their color from near the Round 5 space and adds it to their pool. This Agent is available for the rest of the game.

notes.description: VP tokens that accumulate on Building tiles in Builder's Hall are gained by the player who purchases that Building.

actions.name: End of Round
actions.description: After all Agents have been assigned and Waterdeep Harbor reassignments are complete, the round ends.

steps.description: All players return all their Agents to their Agent Pools.
The player with the First Player marker begins the next round.

notes.description: The Ambassador (if in play) also returns -- it is available again at the Palace of Waterdeep next round.

key_concepts.name: Adventurer Types
key_concepts.description: Clerics (white): needed for Piety Quests. Fighters (orange): needed for Warfare Quests. Rogues (black): needed for Skullduggery Quests. Wizards (purple): needed for Arcana Quests. Commerce Quests often need Gold or a mix of adventurer types.

key_concepts.name: Quest Types
key_concepts.description: Arcana (blue border): magic-focused. Commerce (yellow border): trade-focused. Piety (silver border): religious. Skullduggery (green border): thievery. Warfare (red border): military. Your Lord card grants bonus VP for completing certain Quest types.

key_concepts.name: Plot Quests
key_concepts.description: Plot Quests have a scroll notation and a colored name. When completed, they provide an ongoing benefit for the rest of the game in addition to their reward. Keep completed Plot Quests face-up near your player mat.

key_concepts.name: Mandatory Quests
key_concepts.description: An Intrigue card type that forces an opponent to take a Mandatory Quest (orange border). A player with a Mandatory Quest MUST complete it before completing any other Quest. Mandatory Quests award few VP. When completed, place the card in the Intrigue discard pile.

key_concepts.name: Empty Decks
key_concepts.description: If you must draw from an empty deck, shuffle the respective discard pile to form a new deck. Exception: do not shuffle completed Quest cards back into the Quest deck.

end_game.trigger: The game ends after 8 rounds of play.
procedure: Complete Round 8, including final reassignments from Waterdeep Harbor.
All players reveal their Lord cards.
Perform final scoring for each player.

final_scoring.description: VP already scored during the game from completing Quests (already on the scoring track).
Score 1 VP for each Adventurer cube remaining in your Tavern.
Score 1 VP for every 2 Gold remaining in your Tavern (rounded down).
Score the bonus VP shown on your Lord card, based on the specific conditions it describes.

winning_condition: The player with the most VP after final scoring is the winner. In the event of a tie, the player with the most Gold wins.

tips_for_new_players.description: Check your Lord card early and focus on Quests that match your bonus. +4 VP per matching Quest adds up quickly.
The first player gets 4 Gold, second gets 5, third gets 6, etc. Later players start with more Gold to offset going later.
It is powerful because you play an Intrigue card immediately AND your Agent gets reassigned to another action space at end of round -- effectively getting two actions.
Purchasing Buildings generates ongoing Owner benefits when other players use them. Early buildings pay off more over the course of the game.
Their ongoing benefits compound over the game. The sooner you complete them, the more value you get.
The Castle Waterdeep action space is the only way to get the First Player marker. Going first can be crucial for securing key action spaces.
At the start of each round, 1 VP token is placed on each unsold Building in Builder's Hall. These accumulate and are scored when purchased.
You cannot pass if you have Agents available. You must assign an Agent each turn.
You can complete only 1 Quest per turn, even if you reassign from Waterdeep Harbor.
When reassigning from Waterdeep Harbor, you cannot place your Agent on another Waterdeep Harbor space.
At the start of Round 5, ALL players gain an additional Agent from near the rounds track. Plan for the extra action.
If you have a Mandatory Quest, you MUST complete it before any other Quests. Try to avoid being targeted or keep flexible resources.
You do NOT gain the Owner benefit when you use your own Building -- only when another player uses it.
